data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Market Sentiment Remains Bullish Despite Tariff Ruling
data.description: Gold prices experienced a sharp decline following a Supreme Court tariff ruling, yet bullish sentiment quickly regained dominance, according to Jin10. John Weyey, head of the commercial hedging department at Walsh Trading, noted that despite losing a potential positive factor, gold retains significant upward momentum. Many market participants continue to buy gold due to its ongoing rally, which has persisted over the past six months. Weyey believes that the market will maintain this strategy until a bearish signal emerges. Although the tariff decision has reduced some uncertainty, other risks remain, supporting the continued rise in gold prices.

data.title: Russia Warns of Security Risks from Telegram Use by Troops
data.description: Russia has raised concerns about the use of the Telegram app by its troops on the front line, suggesting that Ukraine could potentially access sensitive information through the platform. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing scrutiny over Telegram, which is widely used by millions of Russians. The warning underscores the potential security vulnerabilities associated with digital communication tools in military operations. As tensions continue, the focus on secure communication methods remains a priority for Russian authorities.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | German Chancellor Friedrich Merz Begins First Visit to China
data.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has commenced his inaugural visit to China, navigating a complex diplomatic landscape. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this trip amid ongoing geopolitical tensions. Merz's visit aims to strengthen bilateral relations and address key issues such as trade and climate change. The German leader's agenda includes meetings with Chinese officials to discuss economic cooperation and global challenges. This visit comes at a time when international relations are increasingly strained, requiring careful diplomacy to foster mutual understanding and collaboration.

data.description:

data.title: Bitcoin Open Interest Falls 54% Since All-Time High
data.description:
According to a report shared by CryptoQuant, Bitcoin’s derivatives market has undergone a significant structural reset following its latest all-time high. Bitcoin Open Interest (OI), primarily tied to futures markets and representing the total dollar value of open contracts, peaked at approximately $47.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-open-interest-falls-54-since-all-time-high/

data.title: EU Fines X Platform €120 Million for Digital Services Act Violation
data.description:
The European Union has imposed a €120 million fine on X Platform, owned by Elon Musk, for allegedly violating the Digital Services Act, according to The Brussels Times. This penalty, issued by the European Commission in December last year, marks the first enforcement action under the EU's digital regulation law, which mandates large platforms to combat illegal content, misinformation, and abuse.

According to Jin10, X Platform has appealed the fine, arguing that the EU's investigation was flawed. In a statement released on its platform, X claimed the decision was based on an incomplete and superficial investigation, citing significant procedural errors, misinterpretation of Digital Services Act obligations, and systemic violations of defense and due process rights.

data.title: SBI Holdings Introduces Blockchain Bond with XRP Payments
data.description: SBI Holdings has unveiled a blockchain-based bond valued at 10 billion yen, specifically aimed at individual investors. According to NS3.AI, this bond provides immediate payouts in XRP tokens equivalent to the investment amount and includes additional XRP rewards as interest over a period of one to three years. This initiative represents a fusion of traditional finance and cryptocurrency, utilizing XRP for both principal and interest disbursements.

data.title: Japan SBI Launches On-Chain Bonds That Instantly Reward Investors With XRP
data.description:
Japan-based financial group SBI Holdings has launched on-chain bonds that grant holders an equivalent amount of XRP. SBI announced the initiative yesterday, marking what it describes as the first-ever on-chain Security Token (ST) bond issuance. Visit Website
source: https://thecryptobasic.com/2026/02/21/japan-sbi-launches-on-chain-bonds-that-instantly-reward-investors-with-xrp/

data.title: Crypto Startups Secure $95 Million in Funding Amid Market Downturn
data.description: Crypto startups have successfully raised $95 million in funding this week, with a focus on prediction markets and AI-powered agents. According to NS3.AI, the ongoing downturn in the $2 trillion crypto market has led to reduced competition from less committed investors, resulting in more favorable deal terms for high-quality projects. Notable funding rounds include Novig's $75 million for a peer-to-peer sports prediction platform, Kresus Labs' $13 million for a simplified Web3 wallet, and PlutonAI's $2.7 million for autonomous AI DeFi agents.

data.title: Santiment: Retail investors continue to increase their Bitcoin holdings, but whale selling may limit the upside potential.
data.description: According to data from the on-chain platform Santiment, since Bitcoin reached its all-time high last October, holdings by small wallets (generally considered retail investors) holding less than 0.1 BTC have increased by approximately 2.5%, reaching their highest supply share since mid-2024. In contrast, the overall holdings of Bitcoin "big players" (whales and sharks) holding 10 to 10,000 BTC have decreased by approximately 0.8%. This structural divergence often leads to volatile price movements, making it difficult to establish a clear trend. Currently, Bitcoin prices are mostly fluctuating around the mid-$60,000 range. Market analysts point out that retail buying can provide some "bottom support" and short-term momentum, but for a sustainable rebound to occur, large investors need to stop distributing Bitcoin or even become net buyers. Analysts believe that Bitcoin currently does not lack retail participation; the key is whether whales will stop selling pressure and shift to structural buying. Otherwise, each rebound may face the risk of being sold off at higher prices.

data.description:

data.title: Vitalik responds to comments about Ethereum's "slow death": Plans four major changes to upgrade the system.
data.description: Recently, community user dasha proposed that "the existing Ethereum should gradually die out through fragmentation (such as L2, application chains), and a pure cyberpunk chain should be rebuilt from scratch based on RISC-V." Vitalik Buterin responded by stating that he rejects the idea of ​​a "slow death" for the existing Ethereum, instead planning to build a "cypherpunk-inspired, non-ugly Ethereum" as a tightly coupled extension of the current system, achieving seamless interoperability, and migrating the existing system via smart contracts within 5 years. Vitalik emphasized that this could be achieved through incremental upgrades similar to the previous "merge" upgrade, followed by four major changes, including state tree optimization, Lean consensus, ZK-EVM verification, and VM changes, to enhance censorship resistance, ZK friendliness, and simplicity.

data.title: Retail Investors Boost Bitcoin Holdings to Record Levels
data.description: Retail investors have significantly increased their Bitcoin holdings, achieving the highest share since mid-2024. According to NS3.AI, this shift comes as whale investors, who possess between 10 and 10,000 BTC, have slightly decreased their share. Analysts indicate that sustained upward momentum in Bitcoin prices relies on renewed buying interest from these large-scale holders to maintain price stability.

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Remove Vietnam from Technology Restrictions List
data.description: Vietnam announced that U.S. President Donald Trump has assured Communist Party chief To Lam of the country's removal from the United States' list of restrictions on advanced technologies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this development as a significant step in the bilateral relations between the two nations. The decision is expected to enhance technological cooperation and trade between the U.S. and Vietnam, fostering a more collaborative economic environment.

data.title: Ethereum Spot ETFs Experience Mixed Net Flows
data.description:
Ethereum spot ETFs saw varied net flows on February 20, according to Odaily. Data from SoSoValue indicates a total net inflow of $17,200 for Ethereum spot ETFs on that day.

The Blackrock ETF ETHA recorded the highest single-day net inflow of $1.7826 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.875 billion. Following ETHA, the 21Shares ETF TETH had a net inflow of $687,700, with its historical total net inflow reaching $17.0904 million.

Conversely, the Fidelity ETF FETH experienced the largest single-day net outflow of $2.4531 million, yet its historical total net inflow stands at $2.465 billion.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $11.141 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 4.68% compared to Ethereum's total market value. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $11.525 billion.

data.title: Missouri Bitcoin Strategic Reserve Bill Advances to Committee
data.description:
Missouri’s proposed Bitcoin Strategic Reserve legislation has taken a procedural step forward, advancing to the House Commerce Committee for review. House Bill 2080 would establish a dedicated reserve fund allowing the state treasurer to hold Bitcoin donations for a minimum of five years.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/missouri-bitcoin-strategic-reserve-bill-advances-to-committee/
